title: Map verification rules
short_description: Ship MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY with nearest-match hints, MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW, MAP.DATE_MISMATCH, and projection-defined checks, each with violating and passing fixtures and concrete fix_hints.
long_description_md:
# Map verification rules

## Why task exists

Product-spec capability 7 names map validity as one of three always-on structural check families: "territory IDs exist in pinned dataset, arrows land inside camera viewport, projections defined." `verify-structural-checks` built REF.*, timing, build-exception checks but explicitly left map validity out of scope, deferred to Phase 4 "once map dataset and territory index exist." That dataset/index now exists (map-geometry-foundation) and node types now exist (map-node-types, dependency). This task closes the loop: without it, a project can reference an unknown territory id, point an InvasionArrow off-screen, or apply a date-mismatched border, and verify stays silent -- the exact class of defect verify exists to catch before render. Ships without this and "verify passes" stops meaning anything for map scenes, which breaks the core honesty claim for the genre-defining primitive.

## Roadmap fit

ROADMAP Phase 4: Map engine, done-when bullet: "Map verify rules live with fixtures: MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY (with nearest-match hints), MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW, MAP.DATE_MISMATCH, projection-defined checks." Word for word this task's scope. Depends on map-node-types (need real Territory/Border/InvasionArrow/MapLabel/MapMarker node shapes to check against) and verification-report-schema (every finding is a namespaced Finding assembled into VerificationReport, same contract verify-structural-checks already builds against -- no parallel report shape here). map-camera-state (sibling Phase 4 task, camera flights + viewport state) is a transitive need for MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW and feeds into map-mini-episode next, not a direct dependency of this task per the given dependency list -- this task consumes whatever camera/viewport-at-t surface map-node-types + the geometry resolver already expose, and does not build camera state itself.

Also leans on the generic nearest-match edit-distance helper verify-structural-checks built for REF.* -- ROADMAP Phase 4 calls this reuse out explicitly ("reusing that mechanism for MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY"). This task wires the map dataset's territory-id namespace into that existing helper, does not reinvent fuzzy matching.

## Scope: the four check families

All findings severity=error (map validity is an always-on structural-tier check per capability 7, same posture as REF.*/TIMING.*/BUILD.EXCEPTION -- a project with a map validity finding never proceeds to render, per verification-report-schema's render-gate policy).

### 1. MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY

Every Territory/Border/InvasionArrow node using a dataset-ref (dataset id + territory id, per data-model.md's Territory subtype: "dataset ref + territory id + date, or inline polygon") gets its territory id resolved against map-geometry-foundation's territory index for the referenced dataset+version. Unknown id -> MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY finding naming node id, dataset, bad territory id. fix_hint reuses verify-structural-checks' bounded edit-distance nearest-match helper, keyed this time on the dataset's live territory-id namespace (plus curated aliases) instead of node ids -- "did you mean FRA?" style, truncated valid-options fallback when nothing close. Inline-polygon Territory nodes (no dataset ref) skip this check entirely -- nothing to resolve.

### 2. MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW

For every InvasionArrow, resolve its full curved path (from/to points or territory-id centroids/boundaries, per data-model.md's InvasionArrow subtype: "from/to (points or territory ids), curvature, head style") through the shared geometry resolver at each sampled time in its visible_window, and compare against the active map camera's resolved viewport (center/zoom -> projected screen bounds) at that same time. Arrow path with zero on-screen overlap for its entire visible window -> MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW, naming node id, t_window, resolved arrow bounding box, resolved viewport bounds. fix_hint gives concrete numbers: "move camera center to include (x,y) or delay arrow entry until camera reaches that viewport at t>=N.N." Partial overlap (arrow visually clipped but head or a segment on-screen) is intentionally not flagged here -- that is a taste/framing call for Phase 5 CONTINUITY./PACING. rules, not a structural map-validity error; this check only catches total off-screen placement, an authoring mistake with no ambiguity.

### 3. MAP.DATE_MISMATCH

For every dataset-ref Territory/Border node carrying an explicit date (or inheriting the scene/project's active date, if the authoring API supports a scene-level date default -- consume whatever map-node-types actually exposes, don't invent a new date-context mechanism here), resolve that date against the referenced territory's validity interval [valid_from, valid_to) from map-geometry-foundation's index. Date outside interval -> MAP.DATE_MISMATCH, naming node id, dataset, territory id, the queried date, and the actual valid interval (open-ended sides rendered as "unbounded"). fix_hint suggests the nearest valid date boundary or, when a same-canonical-identity record exists for an adjacent interval (a historical territory that later becomes a different id), names that alternate territory id as an option.

### 4. Projection-defined checks

Catches map content whose resolved geometry falls outside the active projection's mathematically valid domain before render attempts an undefined transform -- e.g. a point at or beyond an azimuthal projection's antipodal singularity, a regional projection whose declared center is missing/invalid, or a node whose resolved coordinates fail the forward-transform round-trip tolerance map-geometry-foundation's projection layer already asserts internally. Finding code MAP.PROJECTION_UNDEFINED (or MAP.PROJECTION_INVALID_CENTER for the missing/invalid-center case), naming node id, projection kind, offending coordinate. fix_hint: pick a projection whose domain covers the content, or move the regional projection's center. This check calls the projection module's own validity predicate (not a re-derivation of the math) -- verify-side, it's a guard that surfaces the projection layer's own errors as structured findings instead of letting a raw pyproj exception (or worse, silently-wrong output) reach render.

### Entry point and registration

A single callable, e.g. run_map_checks(project) -> list[Finding], running all four families in deterministic order, reusing the territory index / geometry resolver / camera-viewport-at-t / projection-validity surfaces map-node-types and map-geometry-foundation already expose (no re-implementation), registered into the same verify pipeline verify-structural-checks' run_structural_checks feeds -- same VerificationReport assembly, same deterministic-ordering contract, same render-gate behavior. Every one of the four families gets one fixture project that violates it and one that passes it (six-plus fixtures total: at minimum one clean-pass map project exercising all four checks together, plus one violating fixture per code).

## Explicitly out of scope

- Territory index, projections, topology-safe simplification themselves -- map-geometry-foundation, already built.
- Node type definitions, Skia drawing, golden image tests for map nodes -- map-node-types, dependency.
- Camera flight/viewport state itself -- map-camera-state, sibling task; this task only reads the resolved viewport-at-t surface that task (or map-node-types, whichever owns it) exposes.
- Partial-overlap / off-center / poorly-framed arrow taste calls -- Phase 5 CONTINUITY.*/PACING.* rules.
- Cross-scene map-state continuity drift checks (fills/camera/labels persisting incorrectly) -- Phase 5 CONTINUITY.* rules, not this task's date/territory/viewport/projection validity scope.
- The 2-minute mini-episode integration proof itself -- map-mini-episode, next task, consumes these rules but is not built here.
- REF.* node-id/style-role/font-role resolution and the generic nearest-match helper's original implementation -- verify-structural-checks, already built; this task reuses, does not rebuild, that helper.
- anima verify CLI wiring -- pure function delivered here, same posture as verify-structural-checks.

deliverables.description: MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY check: dataset-ref Territory/Border/InvasionArrow nodes resolved against the referenced dataset+version territory index, unknown ids flagged naming node id/dataset/bad id, fix_hint reusing verify-structural-checks' bounded edit-distance nearest-match helper keyed on the live territory-id namespace, inline-polygon Territory nodes skipped. Fixture set: one clean-pass project, one project per violation kind (unknown id with a close match, unknown id with no close match).

MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW check: InvasionArrow paths resolved via the shared geometry resolver across their visible_window and compared against the active map camera's resolved viewport at each sampled time, total off-screen placement flagged with node id/t_window/resolved bounding boxes and a concrete numeric fix_hint, partial-overlap arrows explicitly left passing. Violating and passing fixtures included.

MAP.DATE_MISMATCH check: dataset-ref Territory/Border node dates resolved against the territory index's half-open validity intervals, out-of-range dates flagged naming node id/dataset/territory id/queried date/actual interval with unbounded-side handling, fix_hint suggesting the nearest valid boundary or an alternate same-identity territory id where one exists. Projection-defined checks (MAP.PROJECTION_UNDEFINED / MAP.PROJECTION_INVALID_CENTER) for geometry outside the active projection's valid domain or an invalid regional center, calling the projection layer's own validity predicate rather than re-deriving the math. Violating and passing fixtures for both families.

run_map_checks(project) -> list[Finding] entry point running all four families in deterministic order and assembling results into VerificationReport via the verification-report-schema contract at severity=error, registered into the same verify pipeline verify-structural-checks feeds, exercised end to end against one clean-pass map project covering all four checks plus the full per-code violation fixture library, with full lint/type checks on the new module.

verification.command: pytest -q tests/verification/maps/test_map_checks_integration.py && ruff check src/anima/verification/maps tests/verification/maps && mypy --strict src/anima/verification/maps
